vocabulary.name: Rotten Tomatoes API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain terms and concepts used in the Rotten Tomatoes API.

terms.definition: The percentage of approved critics' reviews that are positive for a given film or TV show. A score of 60% or higher is considered Fresh.

A special distinction awarded to movies and TV shows with a consistent Tomatometer score of at least 75% with a minimum number of reviews. Eligible for theatrical release films must have at least 80 reviews.
A film or TV show with a Tomatometer score of 60% or higher is considered Fresh.
A film or TV show with a Tomatometer score below 60% is considered Rotten.
The percentage of users who have rated the film or TV show positively. Calculated from verified audience ratings.
An Audience Score label indicating that 60% or more of users gave a positive rating (3.5 stars or higher out of 5).
An Audience Score label indicating that fewer than 60% of users gave a positive rating.
A brief editorial summary of what critics think of a movie or TV show, synthesized from all published critical reviews.
A critic designated by Rotten Tomatoes as a major critic, typically from major publications and outlets. Their reviews are highlighted and tracked separately.
A list of movies currently performing at the top of the domestic box office based on ticket sales.
A list of movies currently playing in movie theaters.
A list of movies opening in theaters this week.
A list of movies scheduled to be released in theaters in the near future.
A list of the most rented DVD titles in the current period.
A list of movies that have recently been released on DVD.
A list of newly available DVD titles.
A list of movies scheduled for DVD release in the near future.
The Motion Picture Association of America content rating indicating appropriate audience age: G (General Audiences), PG (Parental Guidance Suggested), PG-13, R (Restricted), NC-17, NR (Not Rated).
The API key required to authenticate requests to the Rotten Tomatoes API, passed as a query parameter.
A query parameter that controls the maximum number of results returned per page in paginated API responses.
A query parameter that selects which page of paginated results to return.
An ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code query parameter that returns localized data when available. Defaults to 'us'.
A query parameter for filtering movie reviews. Values: 'all' (all reviews), 'top_critic' (Rotten Tomatoes designated top critics), 'dvd' (reviews from DVD releases).
The developer program through which access to the Rotten Tomatoes API is managed. Requires an approved application and a paid license agreement.
